description: A hands-on course on Kubernetes Gateway API — the next-generation standard for traffic routing that replaces Ingress. Covers the three-layer resource model (GatewayClass, Gateway, HTTPRoute), role-based ownership for multi-tenancy, path/header-based routing, weighted traffic splitting for canary and blue-green deployments, and zero-downtime migration from Ingress.
summary: Learn the Kubernetes Gateway API from the ground up. Understand why Ingress hits its limits, master the GatewayClass → Gateway → HTTPRoute resource model and its owner-per-layer design, and write production-ready manifests for advanced routing patterns including weighted canary splits and header-based feature flags.
target_audience: Intermediate Kubernetes users — developers and platform engineers who have hands-on experience with Ingress and want to adopt the Gateway API for production workloads.

lectures.title: Kubernetes Gateway API: The Next-Generation Standard for Traffic Routing Beyond Ingress
objectives: Describe the Gateway API resource model (GatewayClass, Gateway, HTTPRoute), understand each resource's responsibility boundary, and explain which Ingress limitations it solves.
Explain how role separation — infrastructure admins owning GatewayClass and Gateway, application developers owning HTTPRoute — benefits multi-tenancy and day-to-day operations.
Write manifests for path-based and header-based routing and for weighted traffic splitting, and apply them to canary and blue-green deployments.
